source.section: 4 - AN/ASQ-228 ATFLIR Targeting Pod > 4.5 - Pointing Methods > 4.5.6 - Targeting Pod Offset

steps.action: In this example, we will start from a Auto Track. The targeting pod will be tracking a reference object.
steps.voiced: Confirm the targeting pod is in Auto Track on your reference point before you offset.

steps.action: Use the TDC DEPRESS control (“ENTER”) to activate OFFSET function. A “+” cross (Offset Cursor) will appear at the center of the reticle.
steps.voiced: Press TDC DEPRESS to activate OFFSET — an Offset Cursor cross appears at the reticle center.

steps.action: Use the TDC (Throttle Designator Controller) Aft/Fwd/Left/Right controls to slew the Offset Cursor over the desired target.
steps.voiced: Slew TDC Aft, Fwd, Left, or Right to place the Offset Cursor on your desired target.

steps.action: Press the Sensor Control Switch in the direction of the FLIR format page to move the targeting pod to the location of the Offset Cursor.
steps.voiced: Press Sensor Control Switch toward the FLIR format page to slew the pod to the Offset Cursor.
